purpose: Dispatch deterministic preview generation jobs for a subject, assign prompt and seed combinations, and persist generated preview records for later review and selection.

endpoints.description: Generate a deterministic batch of preview jobs for the active subject in a session.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Dispatch preview generation batches with deterministic prompt and seed assignment.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use after a subject embedding exists and the workflow is ready to show users an initial preview set.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for final high-resolution fulfillment renders, unconstrained text-to-image generation, or gallery packaging.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Verify the session is in preview-ready state. 2) Call /previews/dispatch with session and subject ids. 3) Store the generated preview identifiers for listing and selection.

failure_modes.condition: Session is not in the previewing state or there is no subject to render
failure_modes.recovery: Advance the workflow into preview-ready state and ensure a subject record exists before dispatching previews

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Gate on explicit session state, enumerate preview prompt ids deterministically, derive repeatable seeds, call an injected inference provider, and persist generation records marked as previews.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Preview batches become hard to debug when prompt ids or seed assignment drift, and empty subject lists should fail fast before any generation work begins.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Keep prompt selection and image generation provider calls separate so preview catalog changes do not break persistence logic.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not infer workflow state from loosely coupled side effects and do not mix preview dispatch with checkout or download bundling.

test_cases.name: reject when session is not previewing
